concerns.text: FR-001 references 'See US-1', but the User Story section uses 'User Story 1' as the header. While the intent is clear, the cross-reference ID format is inconsistent with the section header naming convention used in US-2 and US-3.

All Functional Requirements (FR-002 through FR-006) use the format 'See US-2' or 'See US-3'. The User Stories are titled 'User Story 2' and 'User Story 3'. The cross-references should consistently use the full title or a standardized ID (e.g., 'US-001') to match the section headers exactly, avoiding ambiguity between 'US-1' (implied) and 'User Story 1' (explicit).

Success Criteria (SC-001 to SC-005) reference 'See US-1', 'See US-2', 'See US-3', and 'See Edge Cases'. The 'Edge Cases' section is not a User Story and lacks a formal ID (e.g., 'EC-001'). The reference 'See Edge Cases' is ambiguous compared to the specific 'See US-X' format. Consider assigning an ID to the Edge Cases section or rephrasing the reference.
